    69→# Harness Protocol for Long-Running AI Agents
    70→
    71→## What is a Harness?
    72→
    73→A **harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work effectively on complex tasks that span multiple sessions. Created by **Anthropic** as part of the Claude Agent SDK (formerly Claude Code SDK), the harness addresses a fundamental challenge:
    74→
    75→> "The core challenge of long-running agents is that they must work in discrete sessions, and each new session begins with no memory of what came before."
    76→
    77→The harness acts as a **coordination layer** wrapped around ordinary Claude sessions. It tracks files, tasks, and test results across hours of execution, spinning up fresh conversations whenever one thread gets too bloated to stay coherent.
    78→
    79→## Why Anthropic Created the Harness
    80→
    81→After more than six months shipping updates to Claude Code, Anthropic solved hard problems:
    82→- How agents should manage **memory across long-running tasks**
    83→- How to handle **permission systems** that balance autonomy with user control
    84→- How to **coordinate subagents** working toward a shared goal
    85→
    86→The key insight was finding a way for agents to quickly understand the state of work when starting with a fresh context window.
    87→
    88→## Core Architecture
    89→
    90→```
    91→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    92→│                    Harness Framework                         │
    93→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    94→│                                                              │
    95→│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐  │
    96→│  │  Initializer │ → │    Coding    │ → │    Coding    │  │
    97→│  │    Agent     │    │    Agent     │    │    Agent     │  │
    98→│  │  (Session 1) │    │  (Session 2) │    │  (Session N) │  │
    99→│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘  │
   100→│         │                   │                   │           │
   101→│         └───────────────────┼───────────────────┘           │
   102→│                             │                               │
   103→│                             ▼                               │
   104→│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
   105→│  │              Shared State Files                       │  │
   106→│  │  • claude-progress.txt  (session history)            │  │
   107→│  │  • features.json        (source of truth)            │  │
   108→│  │  • CLAUDE.md            (project context)            │  │
   109→│  │  • git history          (code changes)               │  │
   110→│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
   111→│                                                              │
   112→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   113→```
   114→
   115→## Two-Agent Architecture
   116→
   117→Anthropic recommends a **two-fold approach**:
   118→
   119→### 1. Initializer Agent (First Session)
   120→- Sets up the initial environment
   121→- Creates the harness files (features.json, claude-progress.txt)
   122→- Establishes project structure
   123→- Runs on a specialized prompt optimized for setup
   124→
   125→### 2. Coding Agent (Subsequent Sessions)
   126→- Makes incremental progress on features
   127→- Leaves artifacts for the next session
   128→- Maintains clean code suitable for merging
   129→- Commits progress to git with descriptive messages
   130→
   131→## Core Files
   132→
   133→| File | Purpose |
   134→|------|---------|
   135→| `CLAUDE.md` | Project context, instructions, and conventions |
   136→| `claude-progress.txt` | Session history and current state |
   137→| `features.json` | Feature requirements with pass/fail status (source of truth) |
   138→| `agent_docs/` | Additional documentation for the agent |
   139→| `init.sh` | Script to start dev server and run basic tests |
   140→| `CLAUDE_NOTES.md` | Learnings and gotchas for future sessions |
   141→
   142→## The Work Loop
   143→
   144→When a session starts, the agent follows this loop:
   145→
   146→```
   147→1. READ claude-progress.txt
   148→   → Understand current state and what was done before
   149→
   150→2. READ features.json
   151→   → Identify next feature to implement (passes: false)
   152→
   153→3. RUN init.sh
   154→   → Start dev server, verify app is in working state
   155→
   156→4. IMPLEMENT one feature at a time
   157→   → Focus on single feature, don't try to do everything
   158→
   159→5. TEST the feature
   160→   → Verify it works as specified in steps
   161→
   162→6. COMMIT changes
   163→   → Granular commits with descriptive messages
   164→
   165→7. UPDATE features.json
   166→   → Mark feature as passes: true
   167→
   168→8. UPDATE claude-progress.txt
   169→   → Log what was done for next session
   170→
   171→9. REPEAT or STOP
   172→   → Continue to next feature or end session
   173→```
   174→
   175→## Critical AI Limitations & Mitigations
   176→
   177→> **IMPORTANT**: AI agents have fundamental limitations that this harness addresses.
   178→
   179→### The Blind Spot Problem
   180→
   181→**AI cannot see what it creates.** When building UI/apps, the AI:
   182→- Cannot see the rendered output
   183→- Often assumes blank screens are "working" because code compiled
   184→- Marks features as complete based on code logic, not visual verification
   185→- May claim success when the app shows nothing or errors
   186→
   187→**Solution**: Mandatory visual testing with MCP after every feature.
   188→
   189→### The Stale Knowledge Problem
   190→
   191→**AI training data is outdated.** Claude's knowledge cutoff means:
   192→- Library APIs may have changed
   193→- Configuration patterns may be deprecated
   194→- New versions have different syntax
   195→- Best practices may have evolved
   196→
   197→**Solution**: Always check the current date and search for updated documentation.
   198→
   199→### The Guessing Problem
   200→
   201→**AI tends to guess instead of research.** When stuck, AI will:
   202→- Try random solutions repeatedly
   203→- Waste context on failed attempts
   204→- Not realize when it's going in circles
   205→
   206→**Solution**: Mandatory research protocol for non-obvious errors.
   207→
   208→---
   209→
   210→## Best Practices
   211→
   212→### 1. One Feature at a Time
   213→Don't attempt to complete everything at once. Focus on a single feature, test it, commit it, then move to the next.
   214→
   215→### 2. Clean State Between Sessions
   216→Leave the environment in a clean state:
   217→- No major bugs
   218→- Well-documented code
   219→- Orderly structure
   220→- Tests passing
   221→
   222→### 3. features.json is Source of Truth
   223→Never declare the project complete without all features passing. The `passes` field should only change from `false` to `true` after verification.
   224→
   225→### 4. Commit After Each Feature
   226→Maintain granular git history. Each feature should have its own commit(s).
   227→
   228→### 5. Research Before Guessing
   229→When encountering errors, classify them:
   230→- **Simple errors** (typos, syntax): Fix directly
   231→- **Complex errors** (runtime, config): Research online first
   232→
   233→### 6. Test Before Marking Complete
   234→Always verify the feature works before marking `passes: true`.
   235→
   236→### 7. ALWAYS Check Current Date
   237→Before starting work, verify the current date. **Do NOT assume you are in the year of your training data.** Use the system date to ensure you search for current documentation.
   238→
   239→### 8. Install Dependencies via Commands
   240→**NEVER manually edit package.json/Cargo.toml/requirements.txt to add dependencies.**
   241→
   242→```bash
   243→# CORRECT - Let package manager resolve versions
   244→bun add react
   245→npm install express
   246→pip install requests
   247→cargo add serde
   248→
   249→# WRONG - Manual version specification
   250→# Editing package.json: "react": "^18.0.0"  <- DON'T DO THIS
   251→```
   252→
   253→Why: Package managers install the latest stable compatible versions and handle peer dependencies correctly.
   254→
   255→---
   256→
   257→## MCP Visual Testing (MANDATORY)
   258→
   259→### Why Visual Testing is Required
   260→
   261→AI agents **cannot see rendered UI**. This creates a critical gap:
   262→
   263→```
   264→What AI knows:          What actually happens:
   265→✓ Code compiled         ✗ Blank white screen
   266→✓ No TypeScript errors  ✗ React error boundary
   267→✓ Tests passed          ✗ Component doesn't render
   268→✓ Server running        ✗ CSS completely broken
   269→```
   270→
   271→**Without visual verification, AI will mark broken features as complete.**
   272→
   273→### Visual Testing Protocol
   274→
   275→**See CLAUDE.md section "MCP VISUAL TESTING (MANDATORY)"** for the complete protocol.
   276→
   277→Summary: Use **Playwright MCP** (headless, does not steal focus):
   278→
   279→```
   280→1. mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="..."
   281→2. mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages level="error"
   282→3. mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot filename="..."
   283→4. Analyze screenshot
   284→5. mcp__playwright__browser_close
   285→```
   286→
   287→---
   288→
   289→## Error Research Protocol
   290→
   291→### When to Research vs. Fix Directly
   292→
   293→| Error Type | Example | Action |
   294→|------------|---------|--------|
   295→| **Obvious** | Typo in variable name | Fix directly |
   296→| **Syntax** | Missing semicolon | Fix directly |
   297→| **Import** | Wrong import path | Fix directly |
   298→| **Config** | Webpack/Vite config | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
   299→| **Runtime** | Module not found | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
   300→| **Library** | API changed | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
   301→| **Unknown** | Cryptic error | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
   302→
   303→### The STOP-SEARCH-UNDERSTAND-APPLY Method
   304→
   305→When encountering non-obvious errors:
   306→
   307→```
   308→1. STOP
   309→   - Do NOT try random solutions
   310→   - Do NOT keep making changes hoping something works
   311→   - Recognize when you're stuck
   312→
   313→2. SEARCH
   314→   - WebSearch: "[error message] + [technology] + [current year]"
   315→   - WebFetch: Read official documentation
   316→   - Include the CURRENT YEAR in searches (not your training year)
   317→
   318→3. UNDERSTAND
   319→   - Read the search results carefully
   320→   - Identify the ROOT CAUSE
   321→   - Understand WHY the error occurs
   322→
   323→4. APPLY
   324→   - Implement the CORRECT solution
   325→   - Not a workaround or hack
   326→   - The solution from documentation
   327→
   328→5. DOCUMENT
   329→   - Add to CLAUDE_NOTES.md if it's a recurring issue
   330→   - Note in claude-progress.txt
   331→```
   332→
   333→### Example: Research Flow
   334→
   335→```
   336→Error: "Module '@tauri-apps/api/fs' not found"
   337→
   338→❌ WRONG approach:
   339→   → Try different import paths randomly
   340→   → Downgrade versions blindly
   341→   → Copy code from training data (outdated)
   342→
   343→✅ CORRECT approach:
   344→   1. STOP - This is a config/library error
   345→   2. SEARCH - "tauri 2.0 fs module not found 2025"
   346→   3. UNDERSTAND - Tauri 2.0 moved fs to plugin system
   347→   4. APPLY - Install @tauri-apps/plugin-fs, update imports
   348→   5. DOCUMENT - Note in CLAUDE_NOTES.md
   349→```
   350→
   351→---
   352→
   353→## Session Startup Sequence
   354→
   355→Every coding agent session should begin with:
   356→
   357→```bash
   358→# 1. Confirm working directory
   359→pwd
   360→
   361→# 2. Read progress to understand state
   362→cat claude-progress.txt
   363→
   364→# 3. Check recent git history
   365→git log --oneline -10
   366→
   367→# 4. Read feature requirements
   368→cat features.json
   369→
   370→# 5. Start development server
   371→./init.sh
   372→
   373→# 6. Verify app is working
   374→# (run basic tests or visual check)
   375→
   376→# 7. Begin work on next feature
   377→```
   378→
   379→## Context Management
   380→
   381→The Claude Agent SDK implements automatic **compaction** when context limit approaches. However, you can help by:
   382→
   383→- Using `/clear` between unrelated tasks
   384→- Delegating research to subagents
   385→- Keeping CLAUDE.md concise
   386→- Writing clear, focused commit messages
   387→
   388→## Results
   389→
   390→Using this harness approach, Anthropic has observed:
   391→- Claude maintaining focus for **30+ hours** on complex tasks
   392→- Agents completing multi-week projects across sessions
   393→- Consistent quality without context degradation
   394→
   395→## Official Sources
   396→
   397→- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
   398→- [Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)
   399→- [Claude Code: Best practices for agentic coding](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)
   400→

# Metro Integrated Planning System (Monorepo)

High-Performance Enterprise Integrated Planning System with "Semantic Zoom" UX (Google Earth-style).

## Commands

```bash
bun i              # Install dependencies
bun run dev        # Start development server
bun run build      # Production build
bun run typecheck  # Type check all packages
bun run lint       # Lint all packages
bun run test       # Run tests
```

## Tech Stack

- **Runtime:** Bun
- **Monorepo:** Turborepo
- **App Framework:** Astro (Static/SPA, client-only React)
- **Frontend:** React 19
- **Graphics Engine:** @react-three/fiber, @react-three/drei, three-stdlib
- **State Management:** Custom state-machine (packages/toolkit)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4

## Project Structure

```
system/
├── apps/system/          # Main Astro application
├── packages/
│   ├── domain/           # Business logic, entities, data generators
│   ├── gl-engine/        # WebGL/R3F components, shaders, layers
│   ├── toolkit/          # State machine, utilities
│   └── ui/               # Atomic UI components
└── design/               # Visual reference specifications (Pixel-Perfect Target)
```

## Visual References (design/)

The `design/` directory contains the visual specifications that this system must replicate. See [design/README.md](./design/README.md) for detailed mapping between images and implementation layers.

| Image | View | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| screen-1.png | Macro | Strategic 10-20 year heatmap overview |
| screen-2.png | Meso | Tactical DNA-like allocation strips |
| screen-3.jpg | Micro | Operational task cards with dependencies |
| screen-4.png | Meso | Dense diagonal allocation pattern |
| screen-5.png | Meso | Color-coded resource allocation |
| screen-6.png | Micro | Task details with conflict indicators |
| screen-7.png | UI | Header/sidebar layout reference |

## LOD System (Level of Detail)

The system implements three rendering layers based on camera zoom:

- **Macro (zoom < 0.5):** Procedural shader heatmap - 1 draw call
- **Meso (0.5 ≤ zoom ≤ 2.0):** InstancedMesh with 60k instances
- **Micro (zoom > 2.0):** HTML overlay with Line2 dependency curves

## Documentation

- `docs/` - Domain documentation (requirements, UX specs, research)
- `agent_docs/` - Technical documentation for AI agents
- `design/` - Visual reference specifications

---

# Harness Protocol for Long-Running AI Agents

## What is a Harness?

A **harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work effectively on complex tasks that span multiple sessions. Created by **Anthropic** as part of the Claude Agent SDK (formerly Claude Code SDK), the harness addresses a fundamental challenge:

> "The core challenge of long-running agents is that they must work in discrete sessions, and each new session begins with no memory of what came before."

The harness acts as a **coordination layer** wrapped around ordinary Claude sessions. It tracks files, tasks, and test results across hours of execution, spinning up fresh conversations whenever one thread gets too bloated to stay coherent.

## Why Anthropic Created the Harness

After more than six months shipping updates to Claude Code, Anthropic solved hard problems:
- How agents should manage **memory across long-running tasks**
- How to handle **permission systems** that balance autonomy with user control
- How to **coordinate subagents** working toward a shared goal

The key insight was finding a way for agents to quickly understand the state of work when starting with a fresh context window.

## Core Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Harness Framework                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐  │
│  │  Initializer │ → │    Coding    │ → │    Coding    │  │
│  │    Agent     │    │    Agent     │    │    Agent     │  │
│  │  (Session 1) │    │  (Session 2) │    │  (Session N) │  │
│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘  │
│         │                   │                   │           │
│         └───────────────────┼───────────────────┘           │
│                             │                               │
│                             ▼                               │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │              Shared State Files                       │  │
│  │  • claude-progress.txt  (session history)            │  │
│  │  • features.json        (source of truth)            │  │
│  │  • CLAUDE.md            (project context)            │  │
│  │  • git history          (code changes)               │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Two-Agent Architecture

Anthropic recommends a **two-fold approach**:

### 1. Initializer Agent (First Session)
- Sets up the initial environment
- Creates the harness files (features.json, claude-progress.txt)
- Establishes project structure
- Runs on a specialized prompt optimized for setup

### 2. Coding Agent (Subsequent Sessions)
- Makes incremental progress on features
- Leaves artifacts for the next session
- Maintains clean code suitable for merging
- Commits progress to git with descriptive messages

## Core Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | Project context, instructions, and conventions |
| `claude-progress.txt` | Session history and current state |
| `features.json` | Feature requirements with pass/fail status (source of truth) |
| `agent_docs/` | Additional documentation for the agent |
| `init.sh` | Script to start dev server and run basic tests |
| `CLAUDE_NOTES.md` | Learnings and gotchas for future sessions |

## The Work Loop

When a session starts, the agent follows this loop:

```
1. READ claude-progress.txt
   → Understand current state and what was done before

2. READ features.json
   → Identify next feature to implement (passes: false)

3. RUN init.sh
   → Start dev server, verify app is in working state

4. IMPLEMENT one feature at a time
   → Focus on single feature, don't try to do everything

5. TEST the feature
   → Verify it works as specified in steps

6. COMMIT changes
   → Granular commits with descriptive messages

7. UPDATE features.json
   → Mark feature as passes: true

8. UPDATE claude-progress.txt
   → Log what was done for next session

9. REPEAT or STOP
   → Continue to next feature or end session
```

## Critical AI Limitations & Mitigations

> **IMPORTANT**: AI agents have fundamental limitations that this harness addresses.

### The Blind Spot Problem

**AI cannot see what it creates.** When building UI/apps, the AI:
- Cannot see the rendered output
- Often assumes blank screens are "working" because code compiled
- Marks features as complete based on code logic, not visual verification
- May claim success when the app shows nothing or errors

**Solution**: Mandatory visual testing with MCP after every feature.

### The Stale Knowledge Problem

**AI training data is outdated.** Claude's knowledge cutoff means:
- Library APIs may have changed
- Configuration patterns may be deprecated
- New versions have different syntax
- Best practices may have evolved

**Solution**: Always check the current date and search for updated documentation.

### The Guessing Problem

**AI tends to guess instead of research.** When stuck, AI will:
- Try random solutions repeatedly
- Waste context on failed attempts
- Not realize when it's going in circles

**Solution**: Mandatory research protocol for non-obvious errors.

---

## Best Practices

### 1. One Feature at a Time
Don't attempt to complete everything at once. Focus on a single feature, test it, commit it, then move to the next.

### 2. Clean State Between Sessions
Leave the environment in a clean state:
- No major bugs
- Well-documented code
- Orderly structure
- Tests passing

### 3. features.json is Source of Truth
Never declare the project complete without all features passing. The `passes` field should only change from `false` to `true` after verification.

### 4. Commit After Each Feature
Maintain granular git history. Each feature should have its own commit(s).

### 5. Research Before Guessing
When encountering errors, classify them:
- **Simple errors** (typos, syntax): Fix directly
- **Complex errors** (runtime, config): Research online first

### 6. Test Before Marking Complete
Always verify the feature works before marking `passes: true`.

### 7. ALWAYS Check Current Date
Before starting work, verify the current date. **Do NOT assume you are in the year of your training data.** Use the system date to ensure you search for current documentation.

### 8. Install Dependencies via Commands
**NEVER manually edit package.json/Cargo.toml/requirements.txt to add dependencies.**

```bash
# CORRECT - Let package manager resolve versions
bun add react
npm install express
pip install requests
cargo add serde

# WRONG - Manual version specification
# Editing package.json: "react": "^18.0.0"  <- DON'T DO THIS
```

Why: Package managers install the latest stable compatible versions and handle peer dependencies correctly.

---

## MCP Visual Testing (MANDATORY)

### Why Visual Testing is Required

AI agents **cannot see rendered UI**. This creates a critical gap:

```
What AI knows:          What actually happens:
✓ Code compiled         ✗ Blank white screen
✓ No TypeScript errors  ✗ React error boundary
✓ Tests passed          ✗ Component doesn't render
✓ Server running        ✗ CSS completely broken
```

**Without visual verification, AI will mark broken features as complete.**

### Visual Testing Protocol

**See CLAUDE.md section "MCP VISUAL TESTING (MANDATORY)"** for the complete protocol.

Summary: Use **Playwright MCP** (headless, does not steal focus):

```
1. mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="..."
2. mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages level="error"
3. mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot filename="..."
4. Analyze screenshot
5. mcp__playwright__browser_close
```

---

## Error Research Protocol

### When to Research vs. Fix Directly

| Error Type | Example | Action |
|------------|---------|--------|
| **Obvious** | Typo in variable name | Fix directly |
| **Syntax** | Missing semicolon | Fix directly |
| **Import** | Wrong import path | Fix directly |
| **Config** | Webpack/Vite config | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
| **Runtime** | Module not found | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
| **Library** | API changed | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
| **Unknown** | Cryptic error | **RESEARCH FIRST** |

### The STOP-SEARCH-UNDERSTAND-APPLY Method

When encountering non-obvious errors:

```
1. STOP
   - Do NOT try random solutions
   - Do NOT keep making changes hoping something works
   - Recognize when you're stuck

2. SEARCH
   - WebSearch: "[error message] + [technology] + [current year]"
   - WebFetch: Read official documentation
   - Include the CURRENT YEAR in searches (not your training year)

3. UNDERSTAND
   - Read the search results carefully
   - Identify the ROOT CAUSE
   - Understand WHY the error occurs

4. APPLY
   - Implement the CORRECT solution
   - Not a workaround or hack
   - The solution from documentation

5. DOCUMENT
   - Add to CLAUDE_NOTES.md if it's a recurring issue
   - Note in claude-progress.txt
```

### Example: Research Flow

```
Error: "Module '@tauri-apps/api/fs' not found"

❌ WRONG approach:
   → Try different import paths randomly
   → Downgrade versions blindly
   → Copy code from training data (outdated)

✅ CORRECT approach:
   1. STOP - This is a config/library error
   2. SEARCH - "tauri 2.0 fs module not found 2025"
   3. UNDERSTAND - Tauri 2.0 moved fs to plugin system
   4. APPLY - Install @tauri-apps/plugin-fs, update imports
   5. DOCUMENT - Note in CLAUDE_NOTES.md
```

---

## Session Startup Sequence

Every coding agent session should begin with:

```bash
# 1. Confirm working directory
pwd

# 2. Read progress to understand state
cat claude-progress.txt

# 3. Check recent git history
git log --oneline -10

# 4. Read feature requirements
cat features.json

# 5. Start development server
./init.sh

# 6. Verify app is working
# (run basic tests or visual check)

# 7. Begin work on next feature
```

## Context Management

The Claude Agent SDK implements automatic **compaction** when context limit approaches. However, you can help by:

- Using `/clear` between unrelated tasks
- Delegating research to subagents
- Keeping CLAUDE.md concise
- Writing clear, focused commit messages

## Results

Using this harness approach, Anthropic has observed:
- Claude maintaining focus for **30+ hours** on complex tasks
- Agents completing multi-week projects across sessions
- Consistent quality without context degradation

## Official Sources

- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)
- [Claude Code: Best practices for agentic coding](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)

# Notes for Claude - Future Reference This file contains learnings, gotchas, and solutions discovered during development. Read this to avoid repeating past mistakes. --- ## Technology-Specific Notes ### React Three Fiber (R3F) #### Key Learning R3F v9 has significant API changes from v8. Always check the latest documentation. #### Common Pitfalls - Never use `useFrame` outside of Canvas context - `Html` component from drei requires `transform` prop for 3D positioning - InstancedMesh color changes require `needsUpdate = true` on the attribute #### Correct Usage ```typescript // Correct way to update InstancedMesh colors mesh.instanceColor!.needsUpdate = true; // Correct Html positioning in 3D
Content
``` --- ### Astro + React Integration #### Key Learning Astro's React integration requires specific hydration directives for client-only rendering. #### Important Configuration ```astro ``` #### Common Pitfalls - Don't use `client:load` for R3F - it will cause SSR issues - `client:only="react"` completely skips server rendering - Ensure React 19 compatibility with all libraries --- ### Three.js Shaders (GLSL) #### Key Learning Fragment shaders for heatmaps need careful color interpolation. #### Correct Usage ```glsl // Color ramp for heatmap (Yellow → Orange → Red) vec3 ramp(float t) { vec3 a = vec3(1.0, 0.95, 0.5); // light yellow vec3 b = vec3(1.0, 0.6, 0.0); // orange vec3 c = vec3(0.85, 0.0, 0.0); // red return mix(mix(a, b, smoothstep(0.0, 0.5, t)), c, smoothstep(0.5, 1.0, t)); } ``` --- ### Bun Workspaces / Monorepo #### Key Learning Bun workspaces require proper package.json structure and cross-package imports. #### Important Configuration ```json { "workspaces": ["packages/*", "apps/*"] } ``` #### CRITICAL: Running Scripts in Monorepos **The root `bun run dev` may fail** if the filter doesn't match the package name: ```bash # Root package.json uses filter: "dev": "bun --bun run --filter '@mms/app-system' dev" # The filter MUST match the 'name' field in apps/system/package.json: "name": "@mms/app-system" # ← This is what the filter matches ``` **If root scripts fail, ALWAYS try the direct approach:** ```bash # This ALWAYS works: cd apps/system && bun run dev # Or check what the package is actually named: cat apps/system/package.json | grep '"name"' ``` #### Common Pitfalls - **Never assume root scripts work** - verify first with `./init.sh --no-server` - Root scripts use filters that must match exact package names - Always use `bun add` from the correct directory - Cross-package imports need proper exports in package.json - Run `bun i` from root after adding new packages --- ## Package/Library Notes ### @react-three/drei #### IMPORTANT: Html Component **Correct import**: `import { Html } from '@react-three/drei'` #### Why this matters The Html component bridges WebGL and DOM, allowing real HTML elements to be positioned in 3D space. #### Correct usage ```tsx
Clickable content
``` --- ### three-stdlib #### IMPORTANT: Line2 for Fat Lines **Correct import**: `import { Line2, LineGeometry, LineMaterial } from 'three-stdlib'` #### Why this matters Standard Three.js Line has 1px width limit. Line2 supports variable width. #### Correct usage ```typescript const geometry = new LineGeometry(); geometry.setPositions([x1, y1, z1, x2, y2, z2]); const material = new LineMaterial({ color: 0x3b82f6, linewidth: 2, resolution: new Vector2(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight) }); ``` --- ## Error Solutions ### Error: "No packages matched the filter" (Bun monorepo) **Cause**: Root script uses `--filter` with wrong package name **Solution**: 1. Check actual package name: `cat apps/system/package.json | grep '"name"'` 2. Run directly in the app folder: `cd apps/system && bun run dev` 3. Or fix the filter in root package.json to match the actual package name ### Error: "Cannot use import statement outside a module" **Cause**: Package trying to use CommonJS in ESM context **Solution**: Ensure `"type": "module"` in package.json, use proper imports ### Error: "useFrame must be called inside Canvas" **Cause**: React hook called outside R3F context **Solution**: Move component inside `` or pass state via props/context ### Error: "THREE.WebGLRenderer: Context Lost" **Cause**: GPU memory exhausted or too many textures **Solution**: Dispose unused textures, reduce DataTexture size, check for memory leaks ### Error: InstancedMesh colors not updating **Cause**: Buffer attribute not marked for update **Solution**: Set `mesh.instanceColor!.needsUpdate = true` after changing colors --- ## General Rules 1. **Always research official documentation** before trying to resolve import/ESM errors 2. **Check for alternative versions** of packages for different bundlers 3. **Don't remove dependencies** the user explicitly requested - find the correct way to use them 4. **Test after every change** - don't batch multiple changes 5. **Commit frequently** - small, focused commits are better than large ones 6. **Use bun add** - never manually edit package.json for dependencies --- ## Project-Specific Gotchas ### LOD State Machine The LOD (Level of Detail) is controlled by zoom level: - `zoom < 0.5` → 'macro' (shader heatmap) - `0.5 ≤ zoom ≤ 2.0` → 'meso' (InstancedMesh) - `zoom > 2.0` → 'micro' (HTML overlay) ### Virtual Scroll Sync Sidebar scroll is synced with camera Y using: ```typescript const PX_PER_TRAIN = 24; // DOM → Camera const y = -el.scrollTop / PX_PER_TRAIN; // Camera → DOM el.scrollTop = -viewport.y * PX_PER_TRAIN; ``` ### Spatial Hash Grid MesoLayer uses spatial hashing for O(1) instance picking: - Grid cell size matches task dimensions - Hash key: `${Math.floor(x / cellSize)}_${Math.floor(y / cellSize)}` --- ## Useful Commands ```bash # Install all workspace dependencies bun i # Start development server bun run dev # Type check all packages bun run typecheck # Build for production bun run build # Check git status git status # View recent commits git --no-pager log --oneline -10 ``` --- ## Resources - [React Three Fiber Docs](https://docs.pmnd.rs/react-three-fiber) - [Drei Docs](https://github.com/pmndrs/drei) - [Three.js Docs](https://threejs.org/docs/) - [Astro Docs](https://docs.astro.build) - [Bun Docs](https://bun.sh/docs) - [Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com/docs)

# Prompts para Iniciar Sessões com AI


## Sessões Seguintes (Desenvolvimento)

```
Leia claude-progress.txt para ver o estado atual.
Continue de onde parou seguindo features.json.
Commit a cada feature concluída.
Atualize claude-progress.txt ao final.
Pare.
```

## Primeira Sessão (Setup do Projeto)

```
Leia CLAUDE.md e claude-progress.txt para entender o projeto.
Execute o setup inicial seguindo features.json (setup-001 a setup-008).
Commit a cada etapa concluída.
Pare.
```

## Sessão de Correção (Bugs/Problemas)

```
Leia claude-progress.txt.
Execute pnpm test e pnpm lint para ver o estado.
Corrija os problemas encontrados.
Commit as correções.
Pare.
```

Leia claude-progress.txt.

Pare.

Leia CLAUDE.md e claude-progress.txt.

Pare.

1→## The prompt that started this project
     2→
     3→> **Note:** Visual references are now available at `./design/` - see [design/README.md](./design/README.md) for detailed mapping.
     4→
     5→```md
     6→Act as a Principal Software Engineer and Graphics Systems Architect (WebGL/3D).
     7→
     8→Your objective is to generate the complete and structured source code for a High-Performance "Enterprise Integrated Planning System". Do not treat this as a tutorial or simple outline. The architecture must be robust, modular, and production-ready, aiming for extreme performance (constant 60fps) with large data volumes.
     9→
    10→The system must replicate the "Semantic Zoom" UX of Google Earth, switching visualizations based on camera height. Use the images in the directory `~/Downloads/design` as rigorous visual specifications (Pixel-Perfect Target).
    11→
    12→### 1. Tech Stack & Infrastructure (Monorepo)
    13→The project will be a **Bun-managed Monorepo**, optimized for speed.
    14→* **Runtime:** Bun
    15→* **App Framework:** Astro (Hybrid/SPA Mode). The "shell" of the application is static, the core is a heavy React "Island" - For now, the app must render only on the frontend, without SSR.
    16→* **Frontend Library:** React 19 or a stable version that integrates with the requirements of the required libraries - For React, follow all best practices described on the current site and document (access the updated site and learn updated information before documenting).

## The prompt that started this project

> **Note:** Visual references are now available at `./design/` - see [design/README.md](./design/README.md) for detailed mapping.

```md
Act as a Principal Software Engineer and Graphics Systems Architect (WebGL/3D).

Your objective is to generate the complete and structured source code for a High-Performance "Enterprise Integrated Planning System". Do not treat this as a tutorial or simple outline. The architecture must be robust, modular, and production-ready, aiming for extreme performance (constant 60fps) with large data volumes.

The system must replicate the "Semantic Zoom" UX of Google Earth, switching visualizations based on camera height. Use the images in the directory `~/Downloads/design` as rigorous visual specifications (Pixel-Perfect Target).

### 1. Tech Stack & Infrastructure (Monorepo)
The project will be a **Bun-managed Monorepo**, optimized for speed.
* **Runtime:** Bun
* **App Framework:** Astro (Hybrid/SPA Mode). The "shell" of the application is static, the core is a heavy React "Island" - For now, the app must render only on the frontend, without SSR.
* **Frontend Library:** React 19 or a stable version that integrates with the requirements of the required libraries - For React, follow all best practices described on the current site and document (access the updated site and learn updated information before documenting).
* **Graphics Engine:**
    * `@react-three/fiber` (R3F): Core loop.
    * `@react-three/drei`: Camera Utilities and HTML Overlay.
    * `three-stdlib`: For advanced line geometries (Line2).
* **Toolkit:** Copy and adapt as necessary the package `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/uptime/packages/toolkit` and utilize everything possible that is functioning (verify) - it is mandatory to use the package's state-machine.
* **State Management:** Use the toolkit's state machine.
* **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4 with appropriate theme configuration.
* **Folder Structure (Bun Workspaces):**
    * `packages/domain`: Pure business logic, TypeScript interfaces (Entities: Train, Task, Conflict), and deterministic data generators.
    * `packages/ui`: Atomic UI components (Buttons, Inputs, Cards) decoupled from logic.
    * `packages/gl-engine`: WebGL/R3F specific components (Shaders, Meshes, Controls).
    * `apps/system`: The main Astro application.

### 2. Visual Reference Analysis (copy to the project, they are located in: `~/antonio/mms/design`)
The application must interpret the files in this directory following these heuristics:

* **Macro View (Ref: Image with dense grid/heatmap):** Represents strategic planning (10-20 years). Must display fleet density and "health" via color gradients.
* **Meso View (Ref: Image with colorful diagonal strips/DNA):** Represents tactical planning (2-5 years). Displays high-density individual allocations.
* **Micro View (Ref: Image with text boxes, alert icons, and arrows):** Represents operational planning (4-8 weeks). Displays fine details, critical dependencies, and allows editing.

### 3. Technical Specification of the "Render Engine" (The Heart of the System)
You must implement an `InfiniteCanvas` component that orchestrates three distinct rendering layers based on a state-managed **LOD System (Level of Detail)**.

#### A. Strategic Layer (Macro - Zoom < 0.5)
* **Challenge:** Render 20 years of data without CPU overhead.
* **Solution:** **Procedural Shader Material**.
* **Implementation:** Do not use geometry for the data. Use a single `PlaneGeometry` covering the visible area. Write a **Fragment Shader (GLSL)** that receives a `DataTexture` (encoding train occupancy in pixels) and renders the interpolated heatmap (Yellow -> Red).
* **Goal:** 1 Draw Call. Native GPU performance.

#### B. Tactical Layer (Meso - 0.5 < Zoom < 2.0)
* **Challenge:** 50,000+ distinct task blocks. The DOM would die; normal Meshes would choke.
* **Solution:** **InstancedMesh** with Buffer Attribute Management.
* **Implementation:** A single cube geometry instantiated N times. Colors and positions must be controlled by `InstancedBufferAttribute`.
* **Interactivity:** Implement a "Spatial Hash Grid" or optimized Raycaster to detect which instance is under the mouse and highlight it instantly (changing color on the GPU, not in React state).

#### C. Operational Layer (Micro - Zoom > 2.0)
* **Challenge:** Crisp text, complex SVG icons, rounded corners, and Accessibility.
* **Solution:** **Hybrid Rendering (WebGL + DOM Overlay)**.
* **Implementation:**
    1.  **Connections:** Use Three.js `Line2` (Fat Lines) to draw dependency arrows (smooth Bezier curves) *behind* the tasks.
    2.  **Tasks:** Use the `<Html>` component from `drei` with `transform`. This renders real `divs` positioned in 3D space.
    3.  **UI:** Inside these divs, use full Tailwind components (shadows, flexbox for internal layout, red alert icons if `conflict: true`).
    4.  **Accessibility:** Ensure these HTML elements receive keyboard focus (Tab index) and native click events.

### 4. Synchronization and "Google Earth" UX
* **Orthographic Camera:** Configure dynamic `frustum` to ensure zoom maintains the correct data proportion (time vs. fleet).
* **Virtual Scroll Sync:** The left sidebar (Train IDs) MUST be a separate HTML component (outside the Canvas), but its scroll position (Y) must be mathematically locked to the WebGL camera. If I drag the canvas up, the HTML list descends in the exact same proportion.
* **Time Scrubber:** The footer must be a mini-visualization (Minimap) that controls the main camera's X position.

### 5. Code Execution (Step-by-Step)

Follow this strict order to ensure architectural integrity:

1.  **Domain & Core:** Create `packages/domain` with rich interfaces. Implement a `MockDataGenerator` that creates a complex dependency graph:
    * 50 Trains (Y Lines).
    * 10 years of tasks.
    * Algorithm to inject intentional "Conflicts" (overlapping dates) to be displayed in the Micro View.
2.  **Setup Monorepo & Astro:** Configure the Bun workspace and the Astro app with React integration (`client:only="react"`).
3.  **Base Engine:** Implement the `RenderLoop`, `CameraRig`, and the State Store (using the toolkit) that holds the "WorldState" (zoom level, X/Y offset).
4.  **Layer Implementation (Hardest first):**
    * Implement the `MesoLayer` (InstancedMesh) first to validate mass performance.
    * Implement the `MicroLayer` (HTML + Lines) to validate fine interaction.
    * Implement the `MacroLayer` (Shader) last.
5.  **Visual Polish:** Apply the exact colors from the images (Background: soft corporate gray; Tasks: Deep Blue; Alerts: Danger Red; DNA: Interleaved Orange/Blue).
```

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Production App Migration)

### Migration to Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS

**Decision:** Migrate validated POC (URGENT_POC/canvas.html) to production app (apps/canvas).

**Tech Stack:**
- Vite 5.x (build tool)
- React 18.3.1 (required for BlueprintJS 6)
- BlueprintJS 6.1.0 (UI components from Palantir)
- @react-three/fiber 8.x (pairs with React 18)
- @react-three/drei 9.x (R3F helpers)
- three 0.170.x (WebGL)

**Key Constraint:** React 18 + R3F 8.x pairing is critical. BlueprintJS 6 requires React 18.

**Changes:**
- Updated features.json:
  - Changed product entry to apps/canvas
  - Archived POC (URGENT_POC/canvas.html)
  - Moved F016-F028 to deferred archive
  - Added F101-F117 for production app features
  - Added "canvas" category
- Updated CLAUDE.md:
  - Tech Stack section with production stack
  - Quick Commands for apps/canvas
  - Project Structure for new layout
  - Marked URGENT_POC as archived
- Updated docs/README.md with production app reference
- Updated docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md with R3F/BlueprintJS details
- Updated agent_docs/architecture.md:
  - New tech choices (Vite, React 18, BlueprintJS)
  - Version compatibility matrix
  - R3F version pairing warning

**Next:** Create apps/canvas skeleton (F101)

---

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Consolidation)

### Documentation Update - canvas.html as Single Product

**Changes:**
- Redesigned `features.json` with canvas.html as the single product entry point
- Consolidated all features to target canvas.html (F001-F028)
- Updated `CLAUDE.md` URGENT_POC section to reflect canvas.html focus
- Updated `docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md` implementation details
- Updated `docs/README.md` with current implementation status

**New Feature Structure:**
- F001-F015: Core canvas features (PASS) - pan, zoom, LOD, sidebar, timeline
- F016-F017: Scroll modifiers (PENDING) - Shift+Scroll, Ctrl+Scroll
- F018-F020: Interactions (PENDING) - hover, click, tooltip
- F021-F022: Accessibility (PENDING) - keyboard nav, ARIA
- F023-F028: Advanced features (PENDING) - dependencies, minimap, shortcuts

**Archive:**
- Legacy HTML files (index.html, macro.html, meso.html, micro.html, layout.html) kept as visual references only
- These files are NOT the product - canvas.html is the single entry point

**Rationale:**
- The semantic zoom UX requires a single interactive canvas
- Multiple static HTML files were prototypes to validate individual layer designs
- canvas.html integrates all layers with real pan/zoom interaction

## Session: 2025-12-15 (F017 Pan/Zoom Interactivity)

### F017: Pan/Zoom Interactivity - COMPLETE

**Implemented:**
- Created `URGENT_POC/canvas.html` with full semantic zoom
- Drag-to-pan: Click and drag to move the viewport
- Scroll-to-zoom: Mouse wheel zooms toward cursor position
- Double-click to zoom in 2x at click location
- Zoom controls (+/- buttons) and Reset View button
- LOD transitions working correctly:
  - **MACRO** (zoom < 0.5x): Heatmap with yellow-orange-red gradient
  - **MESO** (zoom 0.5-2x): DNA strips with colored task blocks
  - **MICRO** (zoom > 2x): Detailed task cards with labels and grid
- Timeline header adapts granularity (years → months → weeks → days)
- Sidebar shows visible train IDs synced with viewport
- LOD indicator in header shows current zoom level

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MESO view: Task blocks visible, conflict indicators working
- ✅ MACRO view: Heatmap rendering, all 50 trains visible
- ✅ MICRO view: Grid, task cards with labels
- ✅ No console errors

**Next feature:** F018 (Click/hover interactions)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (F016-BUG Verification)

### F016-BUG: layout.html JS rendering issue - RESOLVED

**Investigation:**
- Visual test via Playwright MCP shows page renders correctly
- Timeline header (Oct 1-8) displays properly
- All task blocks visible (original/red, resolved/blue, unchanged/gray)
- Conflict zone with hatched pattern renders correctly
- Resolution arrow SVG displays properly
- Legend visible and styled correctly
- No console errors

**Conclusion:** The reported "blank screen" issue was a false positive or was resolved in a prior session. Marking F016-BUG as passes:true.

**Next feature:** F017 (Pan/zoom interactivity)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Claude Code Hooks)

### Implemented Harness Enforcement via Hooks

Based on:
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

**Hooks Created:**
- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions
- [x] `protect-package-json.sh` (PreToolUse) - BLOCKS manual package.json edits
- [x] `after-write.sh` (PostToolUse) - Quick feedback after file edits
- [x] `task-type-detector.py` (UserPromptSubmit) - Task-type aware reminders
- [x] `end-of-turn-check.sh` (Stop) - Quality gate at end of turn

**Architecture:**
```
PreToolUse  → BLOCKING (prevent bad actions)
PostToolUse → FAST (quick feedback)
Stop        → QUALITY GATE (typecheck, visual test reminders)
```

## Recurring Issues & Solutions

### Issue: R3F or Three.js import errors
**Solution**: Use `bun add` to install packages, never edit package.json manually

### Issue: Astro hydration issues with React
**Solution**: Always use `client:only="react"` for R3F components

### Issue: Shader compilation errors
**Solution**: Check GLSL syntax, verify uniform names match

### Issue: TypeScript errors with toolkit state machine
**Solution**: Use usePlanningSelector() instead of usePlanning() for type safety

Now I have all the key documentation files. Let me compile a comprehensive analysis of the inconsistencies I've found.

## COMPREHENSIVE DOCUMENTATION INCONSISTENCY REPORT

Based on my analysis of all markdown and JSON files in the system, I've identified significant documentation inconsistencies. Here's the complete inventory:

---

## 1. TECH STACK INCONSISTENCIES

### React & R3F Version Conflicts

| File | Reference | Status | Issue |
|------|-----------|--------|-------|
| **README.md** | "React 19" (line 21) | ❌ INCONSISTENT | Says React 19, but... |
| **CLAUDE.md** | "React 18.3.1" (Production Stack section) | ✅ CORRECT | Production uses React 18 |
| **CLAUDE_NOTES.md** | "React 19 compatibility" (line 46) | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Mixed with React 18 guidance |
| **INITIAL_PROMPT.md** | "React 19 or stable version" (line 16) | ❌ OUTDATED | Original prompt, no longer valid |
| **features.json** | "React 18" (implicit in F101) | ✅ CORRECT | Correct in production specs |
| **claude-progress.txt** | "React 18.3.1" | ✅ CORRECT | Latest session uses React 18 |

**Root Cause:** README.md not updated after migration decision from React 19 → React 18 (because BlueprintJS 6 requires React 18).

**Details:**
```
README.md Line 21: "Frontend: React 19"
CLAUDE.md Tech Stack: "React 18.3.1"
CLAUDE_NOTES.md Line 46: "Ensure React 19 compatibility with all libraries"
```

---

### Build Tool: Astro vs Vite

| File | Reference | Status | Issue |
|------|-----------|--------|-------|
| **README.md** | "Astro (Static/SPA, client-only React)" (line 20) | ❌ INCONSISTENT | Astro framework claimed |
| **CLAUDE.md** | "Vite 5.x" (Tech Stack section) | ✅ CORRECT | Vite is the actual build tool |
| **INITIAL_PROMPT.md** | "Astro (Hybrid/SPA Mode)" (line 15) | ❌ OUTDATED | Original prompt, replaced |
| **features.json** | "Vite + React 18" (F101 description) | ✅ CORRECT | Production uses Vite |
| **CLAUDE_NOTES.md** | "Astro + React Integration" (section header, line 32) | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Documents Astro integration but labeled as legacy |

**Root Cause:** README.md references old Astro setup from INITIAL_PROMPT; migrated to Vite during F101 setup.

**Details:**
```
README.md: "App Framework: Astro (Static/SPA, client-only React)"
CLAUDE.md: "Build Tool: Vite 5.x"
Current Reality: apps/canvas is Vite + React 18, NOT Astro
```

---

### Styling: Tailwind vs BlueprintJS

| File | Reference | Status | Issue |
|------|-----------|--------|-------|
| **README.md** | "Styling: Tailwind CSS v4" (line 24) | ❌ INCONSISTENT | Tailwind, but... |
| **CLAUDE.md** | "BlueprintJS 6.1.0" (Tech Stack) | ✅ CORRECT | Production uses BlueprintJS |
| **CLAUDE_NOTES.md** | "Tailwind CSS v4" (line 255) | ⚠️ PARTIAL | References old styling |
| **features.json** | "BlueprintJS 6.1.0" (F101) | ✅ CORRECT | UI components library specified |
| **INITIAL_PROMPT.md** | "Styling: Tailwind CSS v4" (line 23) | ❌ OUTDATED | Original tech stack |

**Root Cause:** README.md not updated after styling decision: Tailwind → BlueprintJS 6 (Palantir components).

**Details:**
```
README.md: "Styling: Tailwind CSS v4"
CLAUDE.md: "BlueprintJS 6.1.0 (Palantir)"
Reality: Production app uses BlueprintJS for UI, not Tailwind CSS
INITIAL_PROMPT specified Tailwind but was overridden in F101
```

---

## 2. APP ENTRY POINT INCONSISTENCIES

### Primary Product Location

| File | Reference | Status | Issue |
|------|-----------|--------|-------|
| **README.md** | "apps/system" (line 30) | ❌ WRONG | Old app location |
| **CLAUDE.md** | "apps/canvas" (Production App section) | ✅ CORRECT | Current entry point |
| **INITIAL_PROMPT.md** | "apps/system" (line 28) | ❌ OUTDATED | Original structure |
| **features.json** | "apps/canvas" (product.entry) | ✅ CORRECT | Correct in source of truth |
| **CLAUDE_NOTES.md** | "apps/system" (various sections) | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Mixed references |
| **claude-progress.txt** | "apps/canvas" (F101 onward) | ✅ CORRECT | Latest sessions use apps/canvas |

**Root Cause:** README.md and parts of CLAUDE_NOTES.md reference old `apps/system` Astro app which is no longer the product.

**Details:**
```
README.md Line 30: "├── apps/system/ # Main Astro application"
CLAUDE.md: "├── apps/canvas/ # Production React 18 app (Vite + R3F + BlueprintJS)"
Reality: apps/canvas is the production app since F101 (2025-12-15)
```

---

### POC Reference

| File | Reference | Status | Issue |
|------|-----------|--------|-------|
| **CLAUDE.md** | "URGENT_POC/canvas.html - Archived POC" | ✅ CORRECT | Archived status correct |
| **features.json** | "URGENT_POC/canvas.html - archived" | ✅ CORRECT | Archived in source of truth |
| **claude-progress.txt** | "URGENT_POC/canvas.html" | ✅ CORRECT | Reference for production |
| **README.md** | No reference to archived POC | ⚠️ OMISSION | Doesn't mention POC at all |

**Root Cause:** README.md predates POC consolidation and doesn't explain the relationship between POC and production.

---

## 3. PACKAGE NAMING INCONSISTENCIES

### Scoped Package Names

| File | Reference | Status | Issue |
|------|-----------|--------|-------|
| **README.md** | "@mms/toolkit" (line 23) | ✅ CORRECT | Correct scope |
| **CLAUDE.md** | "@mms/toolkit" (references) | ✅ CORRECT | Consistent |
| **CLAUDE_NOTES.md** | "@mms/app-system" (line 86) | ⚠️ PARTIAL | References old package name |
| **INITIAL_PROMPT.md** | "@belt/*" potentially (line 21) | ❌ OUTDATED | References external toolkit |

**Root Cause:** CLAUDE_NOTES.md has legacy content from monorepo troubleshooting with old package naming.

**Details:**
```
CLAUDE_NOTES.md Line 86: "name": "@mms/app-system"
Current Reality: Packages are @mms/domain, @mms/toolkit, etc.
                 apps/canvas doesn't use @mms prefix (it's Vite app)
```

---

## 4. COMMAND/PACKAGE MANAGER INCONSISTENCIES

| File | Reference | Status | Issue |
|------|-----------|--------|-------|
| **PROMPT.md** | "pnpm test" (line 27) | ❌ WRONG | Old package manager |
| **PROMPT.md** | "pnpm lint" (line 26) | ❌ WRONG | Old package manager |
| **PROMPT.md** | "pnpm test:e2e" (line 37) | ❌ WRONG | Old package manager |
| **README.md** | "bun i", "bun run dev" | ✅ CORRECT | Current package manager |
| **CLAUDE.md** | "bun run dev" (Quick Commands) | ✅ CORRECT | Correct commands |
| **CONTINUE.md** | "bun run dev" (implicit) | ✅ CORRECT | References CLAUDE.md |

**Root Cause:** PROMPT.md is Portuguese-language legacy file with old pnpm commands (before Bun migration).

**Details:**
```
PROMPT.md Line 26: "Execute pnpm test e pnpm lint"
PROMPT.md Line 27: "Execute pnpm test:e2e"
Current Reality: Project uses Bun, commands are `bun run test`, `bun run dev`
```

---

## 5. LANGUAGE INCONSISTENCIES

### Portuguese vs English

| File | Language | Status | Issue |
|------|----------|--------|-------|
| **CLAUDE.md** | English | ✅ CORRECT | Primary documentation |
| **README.md** | English | ✅ CORRECT | Consistent |
| **CONTINUE.md** | English | ✅ CORRECT | Consistent |
| **PROMPT.md** | **Portuguese** | ❌ WRONG | "Prompts para Iniciar Sessões com AI" |
| **INITIAL_PROMPT.md** | English | ✅ CORRECT | Original prompt |
| **CLAUDE_NOTES.md** | English | ✅ CORRECT | Consistent |
| **docs/*** | English | ✅ CORRECT | All English |
| **agent_docs/*** | English | ✅ CORRECT | All English |

**Root Cause:** PROMPT.md is legacy file from earlier project phase with Portuguese instructions.

**Details:**
```
PROMPT.md Header: "# Prompts para Iniciar Sessões com AI"
PROMPT.md Content: Entirely in Portuguese
All other files: English documentation
CLAUDE.md rule: "Language: Always write documentation in English"
```

---

## 6. FEATURE ID INCONSISTENCIES

### Next Feature References

| File | Reference | Status | Issue |
|------|-----------|--------|-------|
| **CONTINUE.md** | "Find first feature where passes: false (likely F016)" (line 12) | ⚠️ MISLEADING | F016 is POC, not production |
| **features.json** | F101-F117 are production features | ✅ CORRECT | Correct feature numbering |
| **claude-progress.txt** | "F101: Create Vite app skeleton - COMPLETE" | ✅ CORRECT | Production features active |
| **INITIAL_PROMPT.md** | "setup-001 to setup-008" (line 18) | ❌ OUTDATED | Old setup feature IDs |
| **PROMPT.md** | "features.json (setup-001 a setup-008)" (line 18) | ❌ OUTDATED | Portuguese with old IDs |

**Root Cause:** CONTINUE.md references F016 from archived POC; production features start at F101.

**Details:**
```
CONTINUE.md: "Find first feature where passes: false (likely F016)"
Current Reality: 
  - F001-F015 are POC features (all passing)
  - F016-F028 are deferred POC features (archived)
  - F101-F117 are production features (active)
Next feature should be F102, not F016
```

---

## 7. MONOREPO STRUCTURE INCONSISTENCIES

### Package Locations and Organization

| File | Reference | Status | Issue |
|------|-----------|--------|-------|
| **README.md** | `packages/ui`, `packages/gl-engine`, `packages/domain` (lines 31-34) | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Lists old structure |
| **CLAUDE.md** | `packages/toolkit` (state machine reference) | ✅ CORRECT | Current structure |
| **CLAUDE_NOTES.md** | `packages/gl-engine`, `packages/domain` | ⚠️ PARTIAL | References old packages |
| **INITIAL_PROMPT.md** | `packages/ui`, `packages/gl-engine`, `packages/domain` (lines 25-28) | ❌ OUTDATED | Old monorepo structure |
| **agent_docs/architecture.md** | `packages/toolkit` | ✅ CORRECT | Current structure |

**Root Cause:** README.md not updated after monorepo restructuring. Original prompt listed `packages/ui`, `packages/gl-engine`, `packages/domain`, but current reality is only `packages/toolkit`.

**Details:**
```
README.md Lists:
  ├── packages/domain
  ├── packages/gl-engine
  ├── packages/toolkit
  └── packages/ui

CLAUDE.md Lists:
  └── packages/toolkit (only)

Reality: Check actual directory structure in git
```

---

## 8. DOCUMENTATION SCOPE/COVERAGE INCONSISTENCIES

| File | Scope | Status | Issue |
|------|-------|--------|-------|
| **CLAUDE.md** | Comprehensive (220+ lines) | ✅ CORRECT | Complete project rules |
| **CLAUDE_NOTES.md** | Technical notes & gotchas | ⚠️ STALE | References R3F v9 patterns for v8 setup |
| **README.md** | Quick start + overview | ⚠️ STALE | Not updated after migrations |
| **CONTINUE.md** | Resumption protocol | ⚠️ STALE | References F016 as next |
| **PROMPT.md** | Legacy session prompts | ❌ OBSOLETE | Portuguese, pnpm-era, should be archived |
| **INITIAL_PROMPT.md** | Original prompt | ❌ ARCHIVE | Historical reference only |

**Root Cause:** Multiple files created at different project phases; not consolidated after major migrations (Astro→Vite, pnpm→Bun, React 19→18, etc.).

---

## 9. DATETIME & VERSION INCONSISTENCIES

| File | Date/Version | Status | Issue |
|------|--------------|--------|-------|
| **features.json** | `"lastUpdated": "2025-12-15"` | ✅ CORRECT | Current date |
| **README.md** | No date reference | ⚠️ UNKNOWN | When was this written? |
| **CLAUDE.md** | References "current date: 2025-12-15" | ✅ CORRECT | Accurate in context section |
| **claude-progress.txt** | Multiple dated sessions | ✅ CORRECT | Consistent dating |

**Root Cause:** README.md has no last-updated date, making it impossible to know when it was written.

---

## 10. TECH STACK REFERENCE COMPLETENESS

### Files Missing Key Dependencies

| File | Missing | Status | Issue |
|------|---------|--------|-------|
| **README.md** | Line 22-24: Lists "@react-three/fiber", "@react-three/drei", "three-stdlib", but... | ⚠️ INCOMPLETE | Missing BlueprintJS mention |
| **README.md** | Line 24: "Styling: Tailwind CSS v4" | ❌ WRONG | Should be BlueprintJS 6.1.0 |
| **CLAUDE.md** | Complete tech stack with versions | ✅ COMPLETE | All dependencies listed |
| **features.json** | F101: Lists all dependencies | ✅ COMPLETE | Complete in feature specs |

**Root Cause:** README.md was written before BlueprintJS decision was finalized.

---

## SUMMARY TABLE: INCONSISTENCY SEVERITY

| Severity | Count | Files Affected | Issue Type |
|----------|-------|-----------------|------------|
| **CRITICAL** (Misleads users) | 5 | README.md, CONTINUE.md, PROMPT.md | Tech stack (React, Vite, BlueprintJS) and app location (apps/system vs apps/canvas) |
| **HIGH** (Confusing/stale) | 8 | CLAUDE_NOTES.md, INITIAL_PROMPT.md, PROMPT.md | Package managers (pnpm vs bun), old feature IDs, language mix |
| **MEDIUM** (Partial/incomplete) | 7 | README.md, CLAUDE_NOTES.md, agent_docs/ | Monorepo structure, partial tech stack, outdated patterns |
| **LOW** (Minor omissions) | 3 | README.md, CONTINUE.md, features.json | Missing dates, archive status not clear in all places |

---

## RECOMMENDED FIXES (Priority Order)

1. **README.md** - Update tech stack (React 18, Vite, BlueprintJS 6.1.0) and app location (apps/canvas)
2. **PROMPT.md** - Archive or translate to English; update all pnpm→bun commands
3. **CONTINUE.md** - Change F016 reference to F102 (next production feature)
4. **CLAUDE_NOTES.md** - Clarify Astro section as archived; update R3F version guidance
5. **INITIAL_PROMPT.md** - Mark clearly as "Historical Reference - Do NOT follow"
6. **All files** - Add last-updated dates

All inconsistency details are now documented above.
